reasoning: The scroll_teleport_v01 asset is clearly locatable within 0.5s — the large white circular frame contrasts strongly against both the dark red tile floor and the mid-grey stone tiles. The cyan/teal inner glyph is distinct from other entities (enemy blob, pressure plate). However, the white disc background gives a strong 'pasted UI element' impression rather than a world-embedded item: the smooth circular frame clashes with the pixelated dungeon tile art. The silhouette is readable, but the design reads more like a UI button or tooltip than a diegetic game object. Dark-area visibility is fine due to the bright white surround. No confusion with the enemy or the pressure plate. Echoes-cleanliness is somewhat compromised — the asset has a clean look but the white circle frame is visually heavy and atypical for in-world interactables in this style. Marginal pass: locatable and distinct, but integration fidelity needs refinement.
